subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus:
Fish enter a lake at a rate modeled by the function E given by E ( t ) = 20 + 15 sin . Fish leave the lake at
a rate modeled by the function L given by L ( t ) = 4 + 2 0.1t 2 . Both E ( t ) and L ( t ) are measured in fish per
hour, and t is measured in hours since midnight ( t = 0 ).

parts.prompt:
How many fish enter the lake over the 5-hour period from midnight ( t = 0 ) to 5 A.M. ( t = 5 ) ? Give your
answer to the nearest whole number.

scoring_points.description: Correct estimate or setup with supporting work
Correct interpretation or answer with units

parts.prompt:
What is the average number of fish that leave the lake per hour over the 5-hour period from
midnight ( t = 0 ) to 5 A.M. ( t = 5 ) ?

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (b), point 1
See College Board scoring guidelines for part (b), point 2

parts.prompt: At what time t, for 0 £ t £ 8, is the greatest number of fish in the lake? Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct identification of extremum or critical value

required_elements: Critical point analysis or sign change

scoring_points.description: Correct justification using first derivative test

parts.prompt:
Is the rate of change in the number of fish in the lake increasing or decreasing at 5 A.M. ( t = 5 ) ? Explain
your reasoning.

scoring_points.description: Correct average value or rate integral
